title: Bergamot and lime room spray
subtitle: A bright, citrus-forward room fragrance spray with bergamot and lime essential oils
excerpt: Bergamot and lime are both citrus top notes that give this room spray a bright, uplifting character. Isopropyl alcohol dissolves the essential oils into the water phase and acts as a preservative. Shake before each use as the oils separate from the water slightly on standing. Yield is 200 ml in a spray bottle.
sourceNotes: Room spray formulation: essential oils are not water-soluble; isopropyl alcohol (or high-proof alcohol) acts as a solubiliser, dispersing the oils into the water phase to form a stable mist. At 10% isopropyl alcohol in this blend, the oils are partially solubilised; some separation on standing is expected; shake before use. Bergamot EO (Citrus bergamia, cold-pressed peel): phototoxic on skin (contains bergapten); safe in a room spray that is not applied to skin. Lime EO (Citrus aurantifolia, cold-pressed peel): phototoxic on skin; safe in a room spray. Both toxic to cats if concentrated; diluted and sprayed in a room and allowed to dry is a low exposure scenario; note the caution.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. One spray-bottle per batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Shake before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed spray bottle. The isopropyl alcohol acts as a preservative. Shake before use. Discard if the spray develops an unusual smell beyond the bergamot-lime character.

glossaryTerms.definition: An ingredient that helps water-insoluble substances (such as essential oils) disperse evenly in water. In a room spray, isopropyl alcohol acts as a partial solubiliser: it dissolves the essential oils and disperses them in the water phase when the bottle is shaken. The oils will slowly separate again on standing; shaking restores the dispersion.

content.text: Essential oils do not dissolve in water without a

. In this room spray, isopropyl alcohol dissolves the bergamot and lime oils and disperses them in the water phase. Some separation will occur on standing; a shake before each spray redistributes the oils and ensures a fine, even mist. Bergamot and lime are bright citrus top notes: they are sharp and uplifting on first spray and fade within 30 to 60 minutes in a room. Both essential oils are phototoxic on skin; this spray is for rooms, not skin. Do not spray directly onto skin.

items.prepNote: 90%+ grade; acts as solubiliser and preservative; do not substitute with rubbing alcohol blends
Citrus-floral top note; phototoxic on skin
Bright citrus top note; phototoxic on skin

content.text: In the spray bottle or a jug, add the isopropyl alcohol. Add the bergamot and lime essential oils to the alcohol and swirl to combine; the alcohol will dissolve the oils. Add the distilled water and shake or stir to mix. Pour into the spray bottle if not already in it. Label with the batch date.

Shake the bottle before each use. Spray 3 to 4 pumps into the centre of a room or towards the ceiling and allow the mist to fall. Do not spray directly onto fabrics, furniture, or people. Do not spray near open flames; isopropyl alcohol is flammable.
Store in a sealed spray bottle away from heat and direct sunlight. Keeps 12 months. The isopropyl alcohol preserves the blend. Store away from open flames.
